author: nikigammer22
content:
Subject: [Feedback & Intro] Sub-500ms Non-Custodial POS/E-Com Settlement Layer (EIP-7702 + Hardware Enclaves)

Hey,

I wanted to share a project we’ve been engineering and get some honest feedback from the devs and builders in this group.

We are building a white-label, 100% Non-Custodial B2B settlement infrastructure designed to bring an "Apple Pay-like" payment experience to crypto wallets at physical POS terminals and online checkouts. Tech Stack & Key Architectural Highlights:

Sub-500ms Approval: In-memory execution layer combined with EIP-7702 session delegation and dynamic RAM-locking.

Hardware-Grade Security: Keys are secured via smartphone Secure Enclaves / WebAuthn (Face ID / Passkeys).

Automated Safeguards: 24/7 gateway monitoring, automated revert/failover protocols via WAL (Write-Ahead-Logging), and native real-time tax/receipt export.

Merchant-Ready: Native compliance alignment (GDPR & MiCA) + ZK-proofs for privacy-preserving AML screening. Zero gas/FX fees for end-users. 

Current Stage & Next Steps:

Dev Status: Core MVP, API Gateways, In-Memory Engine, and Developer Sandbox are functional. Smart contract audits (CertiK/OpenZeppelin) are scheduled for the next phase.

Go-To-Market: Preparing for our closed pilot phase (50,000 initial users across major EVM/Solana wallets).

 Why I’m Posting Here:

Dev Feedback: We’d love your technical critique on session key management and latency optimization for POS gateways.

Technical Partners: If you are working on Web3 payment infrastructure, or if you have direct connections to wallet BD teams (Rabby, Rainbow, Phantom), let’s talk!

Talent / Contributors: We are looking to expand our core team (Senior Go / Rust / Smart Contract Engineers) for the upcoming steps.

Check out our 1-Page Executive Summary (happy to share via DM). Feel free to roast the architecture or drop your thoughts below!

DMs are open!
Lg Niki
